text:
Market today: Rough tape for your book. A global AI sell-off is hammering chip names - exactly where you live. That's the real driver today, not macro noise. Margin makes every down-tick cost more.

Your holdings: PLTR is the sharpest pain - down 12.77% and flagged SELL. That's real money on margin; the stop at $85 is far below current price but the SELL flag means the scanner wants you acting now, not waiting. AVGO is your largest position, down 12.45% and flagged TRIM - chip sell-off pressure hits this one directly; size makes it your biggest dollar risk. SPAL is down 14.48% - this is a 2x leveraged ETF that decays every day you hold it in a down trend; it's bleeding from both sides. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no trading history; margin amplifies any gap-down sharply.

Watch: (1) Whether the chip sell-off deepens or stabilizes - SMH is your tell. (2) Any Fed rate-hike chatter from PCE data; that headline is real and would crush growth/tech names further.

Bottom line: Your two biggest losers are also your two biggest flags - PLTR and AVGO need a decision today, not tomorrow.
